title: alcea wilhelminae var. sublacerata
intro: Alcea wilhelminae var. sublacerata, commonly known as the Mexican hollyhock, is a perennial plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Alcea wilhelminae var. sublacerata include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be administered internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
